reasoning: The Elo rating is updated monthly by FIDE and Wikipedia copies this list. The November 1 list has Shakhriyar Mamedyarov at 2742. On November 9, his rating on Wikipedia will be 2742, and on November 16 it will still be the exact same November list (2742). Therefore, the probability of it being exactly 1% higher (~2770) on Nov 16 is virtually zero. For the Dec 9 and Feb 7 dates, he would need to gain roughly 28 Elo points. Given his K-factor of 10, this requires scoring roughly +2.8 expected points over his performance in upcoming events like the FIDE World Cup (i.e. an incredible streak of dominant wins over other grandmasters without draws). For a 40-year-old super GM, a +28 point rating surge in 1 to 3 months is exceedingly rare. Many top GMs actually lose standard rating points in knockout events like the World Cup due to draws against lower-rated opponents. Thus, the chances remain exceedingly slim across all dates.
